project.name: LLM Gateway
project.description: LLM API security proxy gateway — intercepts, scans, and audits all OpenAI-compatible API requests

sprints.name: Phase 9 - Admin Dashboard Bug Fix Sprint
sprints.goal: Fix all 18 bugs documented in admin/e2e/BUGS.md: 2 High functional bugs, 7 Medium functional bugs, 4 Low functional bugs, 4 Accessibility bugs, and eliminate code quality issues by extracting shared components.

features.title: Fix 401 redirect blocking login error display
features.description: Bug #1 (High): apiClient hard-redirects on 401 via window.location.href before React error handlers run. Login page cannot show 'Token invalid' error. Fix: Remove the hard redirect from api-client.ts request(); let the calling code (login page, auth-guard) handle 401s via the thrown error. The auth-guard in use-auth should handle token clearing and redirect.
acceptance_criteria: Given invalid token on login page, when form is submitted, then 'Token invalid' error message is displayed (no hard redirect)
Given an authenticated session that expires, when any API call returns 401, then the app redirects to login via React router (not window.location)
Given the login error flow, when error occurs, then the error state is set and the red error text is visible
features.technical_notes: In api-client.ts:22-25, remove the window.location.href redirect. Instead, just throw the error and let callers handle it. The use-auth.ts login() already catches errors. For other pages, the 401 should be caught by React Query's onError or a query client interceptor that calls clearToken() + navigate('/login').

features.title: Fix fixed header overlapping page content
features.description: Bug #2 (High): Fixed header (h-16, z-30) overlaps page content. The navbar uses `fixed top-0 right-0` without `left-0`, so it doesn't span full width. Page content below needs pt-16 padding to avoid being hidden behind the header. 'Create Key' button is partially blocked.
acceptance_criteria: Given the admin layout, when page renders, then the header spans the full width (left-0 to right-0)
Given any page with a top action button, when scrolled to top, then the button is fully visible and clickable (not behind header)
Given the layout, when content scrolls, then the header remains fixed at top without overlapping interactive elements
features.technical_notes: Fix navbar.tsx: change `fixed top-0 right-0` to `fixed top-0 inset-x-0` (or `left-0 right-0`). Ensure main content area has proper top padding (pt-16) to offset the fixed header height. Check App.tsx layout structure for the content wrapper padding.

features.title: Fix inverted latency delta color on Overview
features.description: Bug #3 (Medium): Latency delta color direction is inverted in overview.tsx. Higher latency (worse) shows green with up arrow, implying improvement. Should show red for increased latency and green for decreased.
acceptance_criteria: Given avg latency increased vs previous period, when delta is displayed, then it shows red color with up arrow
Given avg latency decreased vs previous period, when delta is displayed, then it shows green color with down arrow
Given no change in latency, when delta is displayed, then neutral color is shown
features.technical_notes: In overview.tsx, find the latency KPI card delta rendering. The color logic is likely swapped: increase should be text-red-500, decrease should be text-green-500. Swap the color condition for the latency metric specifically (unlike cost/requests where up is good).

features.title: Add error UI for CSV export on Audit page
features.description: Bug #4 (Medium): CSV export handleExport catches no errors — the catch is missing so errors from exportAuditCsv are silently swallowed. User sees spinner disappear with no feedback. Bug #5 (Medium): Export also accumulates all rows in memory before generating CSV, risking OOM for large datasets.
acceptance_criteria: Given export is triggered and backend returns error, when export fails, then an error toast/message is displayed to the user
Given export succeeds, when large dataset is exported, then CSV downloads successfully
Given the export function, when called with thousands of rows, then memory usage is bounded (chunked/streaming approach or row limit)
features.technical_notes: In audit.tsx handleExport: add a try/catch that sets an error state and displays a toast or inline error. In export.ts: add a max row limit (e.g., 10000) or implement streaming via Blob constructor with incremental chunks to avoid OOM. Display a warning if limit is hit.

features.title: Wrap clipboard.writeText in try/catch
features.description: Bug #6 (Low): navigator.clipboard.writeText in keys.tsx is not wrapped in try/catch. On non-HTTPS, non-localhost origins it throws DOMException and breaks the copy flow.
acceptance_criteria: Given admin on non-HTTPS origin (not localhost), when copy button is clicked, then no unhandled exception occurs
Given copy succeeds, when button is clicked, then visual feedback (checkmark) is shown
Given copy fails, when button is clicked, then a graceful fallback or error indicator is shown
features.technical_notes: In keys.tsx around line 25, wrap the navigator.clipboard.writeText call in try/catch. On failure, could show a brief error tooltip or fall back to selecting text in an input for manual copy.

features.title: Fix empty rate limit fields sending 0 instead of omitting
features.description: Bug #7 (Low): Empty rate limit fields in create-dialog.tsx and edit-sheet.tsx send {rpm:0, tpm:0, rpd:0} which effectively disables rate limits. Should omit these fields or send undefined to use server defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Given create key dialog with empty RPM/TPM/RPD fields, when submitted, then the request body omits rate limit fields or sends null
Given edit sheet with empty rate limit fields, when saved, then existing rate limits are preserved (not reset to 0)
Given create key dialog with filled rate limit values, when submitted, then the correct values are sent
features.technical_notes: In create-dialog.tsx:30-36 and edit-sheet.tsx:33-37, change the payload construction to only include rpm/tpm/rpd when the field has a non-empty value. Use conditional spreading: ...(rpm ? { rpm } : {}) pattern.

features.title: Fix pie chart legend color mismatch for non-standard statuses
features.description: Bug #8 (Low): Pie slice and legend colors can mismatch for non-standard status names. Chart uses DEFAULT_COLORS[index] but legend falls back to hardcoded '#6366f1'.
acceptance_criteria: Given pie chart with non-standard status name, when rendered, then legend dot color matches the corresponding pie slice color
Given pie chart with standard names (success/error/blocked), when rendered, then both chart and legend use the same named colors
features.technical_notes: In pie-chart.tsx, the legend map needs access to the same index-based DEFAULT_COLORS logic as the pie cells. Pass the index to the legend rendering or compute color the same way: COLORS[entry.name] ?? DEFAULT_COLORS[data.indexOf(entry) % DEFAULT_COLORS.length].

features.title: Make DataTable empty state text configurable
features.description: Bug #9 (Low): DataTable component has hardcoded 'No keys found' text. Should accept an optional emptyMessage prop with a generic default.
acceptance_criteria: Given DataTable with no data and no emptyMessage prop, when rendered, then generic 'No data found' text is shown
Given DataTable with emptyMessage='No audit logs found', when rendered with no data, then the custom message is shown
Given all existing DataTable usages, when upgraded, then they can pass appropriate context-specific messages
features.technical_notes: Add emptyMessage?: string prop to DataTableProps with default 'No data found'. Update the empty state td to use this prop. Update callers (keys.tsx, audit.tsx, etc.) to pass appropriate messages.

features.title: Remove formatUsd dead code branch
features.description: Bug #10 (Low): formatUsd() in utils.ts has a dead code branch — if (n < 1) is identical to the final return statement. Both produce $${n.toFixed(2)}.
acceptance_criteria: Given formatUsd(0.5), when called, then output is still '$0.50' (no behavior change)
Given formatUsd code, when reviewed, then there are no redundant branches
features.technical_notes: Remove the `if (n < 1) return \`$${n.toFixed(2)}\`;` line since it's identical to the final return. Keep the `n < 0.01 && n > 0` branch which is unique.

features.title: Fix provider health bar to show health not latency width
features.description: Bug #11 (Low): Key health bar width is proportional to latency — high-latency keys get wider bars, which is misleading for a 'Key Health' visualization. Bar should represent health status (uptime/success rate), not latency.
acceptance_criteria: Given healthy key with low latency, when health bar renders, then it shows a full-width green bar
Given unhealthy key with high latency, when health bar renders, then it shows a short red bar
Given mixed health keys, when bars render, then healthy keys show full bars regardless of latency
features.technical_notes: In providers.tsx KeyHealthBars: change bar width logic to represent health (e.g., 100% for healthy, proportional to error rate for unhealthy). The color already indicates health (green/red/yellow). The latency value should remain as text label, not drive the bar width.

features.title: Add label-input associations for accessibility
features.description: Bug #12 (Medium): label elements in login.tsx, create-dialog.tsx, edit-sheet.tsx, and audit.tsx are not associated with inputs via htmlFor/id attributes. Screen readers cannot announce the label when the input is focused.
acceptance_criteria: Given login form, when screen reader focuses the token input, then 'Admin Token' label is announced
Given create key dialog, when screen reader focuses any input, then the corresponding label is announced
Given edit sheet, when screen reader focuses any input, then the corresponding label is announced
Given audit page filters, when screen reader focuses date/status inputs, then corresponding labels are announced
features.technical_notes: Add htmlFor/id pairs to all label/input combinations: login.tsx (token input), create-dialog.tsx (name, rpm, tpm, rpd), edit-sheet.tsx (name, rpm, tpm, rpd), audit.tsx (status, model, date range filters). Use descriptive unique IDs like 'key-name-input', 'key-rpm-input', etc.

features.title: Add focus trap to all modals and drawers
features.description: Bug #13 (Medium): No focus trap in any dialog or drawer — Tab key navigates to elements behind the overlay. Bug #14 (Medium): Pressing Escape does not close any dialog or drawer. Both issues affect create-dialog, edit-sheet, revoke-dialog, and detail-drawer.
acceptance_criteria: Given any open dialog/drawer, when Tab key is pressed repeatedly, then focus cycles within the overlay only
Given any open dialog/drawer, when Escape key is pressed, then the dialog/drawer closes
Given dialog/drawer closed, when Tab is pressed, then normal page focus flow resumes
features.technical_notes: Implement a simple focus trap hook (useFocusTrap) or add onKeyDown={handleEscape} + ref-based focus management to each overlay component. For focus trap: on mount, query all focusable elements, on Tab/Shift+Tab cycle within. For Escape: add keydown listener that calls onClose. Apply to: create-dialog.tsx, edit-sheet.tsx, keys.tsx (revoke dialog), audit/detail-drawer.tsx.

features.title: Add aria-labels to icon-only buttons
features.description: Bug #15 (Low): Icon-only buttons in login.tsx (eye toggle) and keys.tsx (copy, edit, revoke actions) are missing aria-label attributes. Screen readers announce them as unlabeled buttons.
acceptance_criteria: Given login page eye toggle button, when screen reader encounters it, then 'Toggle password visibility' is announced
Given keys page copy button, when screen reader encounters it, then 'Copy key' is announced
Given keys page edit button, when screen reader encounters it, then 'Edit key' is announced
Given keys page revoke button, when screen reader encounters it, then 'Revoke key' is announced
features.technical_notes: Add aria-label to: login.tsx eye/eyeoff toggle button, keys.tsx copy button, keys.tsx edit (pencil) icon button, keys.tsx revoke (trash) icon button, navbar.tsx logout button and mobile menu button.

features.title: Extract shared StatusBadge component
features.description: Bug #17 (Low): StatusBadge component is duplicated verbatim in overview.tsx and audit.tsx. Extract to a shared component file.
acceptance_criteria: Given a new shared component file, when StatusBadge is imported in overview.tsx and audit.tsx, then both pages render identically to before
Given the old inline definitions, when removed, then no duplicate code remains
features.technical_notes: Create admin/src/components/status-badge.tsx (or similar). Move StatusBadge from overview.tsx to this file. Import in both overview.tsx and audit.tsx. The component is identical in both files so extraction is straightforward.

features.title: Extract shared InjectionScoreBar component
features.description: Bug #18 (Low): InjectionScoreBar logic is duplicated 4 times across overview.tsx, audit.tsx, security.tsx, and detail-drawer.tsx. Extract to a shared component.
acceptance_criteria: Given a new shared component file, when InjectionScoreBar is imported in all 4 files, then all pages render identically to before
Given the 4 inline implementations, when removed, then no duplicate code remains
features.technical_notes: Create admin/src/components/injection-score-bar.tsx. Find the InjectionScoreBar pattern in all 4 files, unify into a single component with props for score (number) and optional size/threshold config. Import in all 4 consumer files.

features.title: Remove unused npm dependencies
features.description: Bug #16 (Low): admin/package.json lists date-fns and class-variance-authority as dependencies but they are never imported in the codebase.
acceptance_criteria: Given admin/package.json, when reviewed, then only actually-used dependencies are listed
Given the admin build, when pnpm install && pnpm build runs, then build succeeds without errors
features.technical_notes: Run grep/search to confirm date-fns and class-variance-authority are truly unused. If confirmed, remove from package.json dependencies and run pnpm install to update lockfile. Verify build still works.
